architecture.summary: Hyperbolic Sphere Neural Network (HSphNN) extends vector embeddings into sphere embeddings to explicitly encode set-theoretic relations through sphere boundaries in hyperbolic vector space. Part-whole relations are represented as sphere configurations, enabling symbolic reasoning over Aristotelian syllogisms. The network constructs a target sphere configuration through neighbourhood transitions, achieving reasoning outputs with explicit set-theoretic semantics alongside traditional neural computation. HSphNN can be integrated with external LLMs (ChatGPT variants) via prompting to verify and improve their reasoning.

biblio.title: Neural Reasoning for Sure Through Constructing Explainable Models
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: HSphNN constructs a sphere configuration as its semantic model for any consistent set-theoretic relation within a constant number M of epochs, with M=1 for HSphNN
HSphNN is the first reasoning-for-sure neural network for all types of Aristotelian syllogistic reasoning
HSphNN successfully checks both decisions and explanations of ChatGPT (gpt-3.5-turbo and gpt-4o) without errors
HSphNN improves the performance of gpt-3.5-turbo from 46.875% to 58.98% through prompts
HSphNN improves the performance of gpt-4o from 82.42% to 84.76% through prompts
HSphNN achieves the symbolic level rigour of syllogistic reasoning

Set-theoretic relations are introduced explicitly and seamlessly into neural networks by extending vector embedding into sphere embedding, so that part-whole relations can explicitly encode set-theoretic relations through sphere boundaries in the vector space
